commit.message:
test(admin-ui): cover trimmed request log tag filters

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(identity): address review feedback after rebase

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime (#935)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

* feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime

Replace 1s polling with Supabase Realtime subscription on import_jobs
for instant progress updates, with a 5s polling fallback for resilience.

- Add adaptive progress reporter cadence (~20 writes/job vs fixed 25-row)
- Enable Realtime on import_jobs table (idempotent migration)
- Show indeterminate progress bar during initial data loading phase
- Handle terminal transitions (validating → preview_ready) with row fetch
- Invalidate in-flight polls when Realtime updates arrive
- Add reduced-motion support for progress animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address CodeRabbit review findings

- Add background: true to terminal transition refreshJob call
- Handle Realtime subscribe errors (CHANNEL_ERROR/TIMED_OUT)
- Assert aria-valuenow absent for indeterminate progress
- Tighten adaptive write count bounds (20-27 instead of 10-35)
- Add tests for subscribe error handling and background flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(migrations): wire Realtime callbacks into migration jobs hook

Connect onRealtimeJobUpdate and onRefreshRequestIdBump from
useMigrationJobs into useMigrationJobPolling so Realtime payloads
merge into the jobs list via mergeJobs and invalidate stale polls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address stale ref, aria-valuenow, test perf, and migration guard

- Terminal transition uses includeJob: true so refreshJob reads the fresh
  server status instead of stale selectedJobRef (race condition fix)
- Forward value prop to ProgressPrimitive.Root so Radix sets aria-valuenow
  on determinate progress bars and omits it for indeterminate
- Replace 5823-row iteration with boundary-based calls in adaptive step
  test (exact 26 writes assertion, much faster)
- Guard migration against missing supabase_realtime publication so it's
  safe in environments without Realtime

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): use exact counts and monotonic sequences in progress reporter tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(virtual-camera): stabilize demand lifecycle and release flow

Summary:
- gate host preview on real capture demand so the menubar app stays idle
  without an open window or external virtual camera consumer
- move early wake-up to authorizedToStartStream, add demand inspection and
  Chromium/native probe harnesses, and harden the background test scripts
- add local install and DMG publishing skills, bump the app and sysext to
  0.1.0 (10), and refresh the manual release asset workflow

Rationale:
- the host was keeping the physical camera alive after UI teardown because
  preview startup and shutdown were still coupled to appearance state
- the sysext wake-up path was too noisy and a stale extension process hid
  the real behavior until the bundle version forced a clean restart
- reproducible local probes and a repeatable DMG flow are needed so the
  fix can be validated and shipped without manual QA loops

Tests:
- xcodebuild -project Reframe.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' -derivedDataPath build/test test
- ./.agents/skills/local-test/scripts/build_and_install.sh
- build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe print-demand
- CLI_PATH="$PWD/build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe" APP_PATH='/Applications/Reframe.app' ./Scripts/run-chromium-background-virtual-camera-probe.sh
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/build_release_dmg.sh /Applications/Reframe.app build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/publish_release_asset.sh v0.1.0 build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add standard open source project metadata

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: separate file stream aborts from fusillade errors (#183)

## Summary
- add `FileStreamItem::Abort` and `FileStreamResult` so
producer-initiated aborts are no longer surfaced as fusillade validation
errors
- update `create_file_stream` to return `Result<FileStreamResult>` and
roll back cleanly on abort
- deprecate `FileStreamItem::Error(String)` while preserving the old
path for compatibility, and update tests to cover abort behavior

## Why
Producer-side aborts are not owned by fusillade. Treating them as
`Err(FusilladeError::ValidationError(String))` forced callers to
round-trip their own failures through fusillade, erased type
information, and encouraged fragile string matching. This change
separates producer intent (`Abort`) from actual fusillade failures and
makes the ownership boundary explicit.

## Context
Fixes
https://linear.app/doubleword/issue/COR-14/clean-abort-signaling-in-fusillade

## Local verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check --lib`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings`

I also attempted to run the DB-backed CI path locally, including
starting PostgreSQL with the system binaries and Debian cluster tooling,
but this sandbox blocks the required privilege transition to the
`postgres` user, so full local `cargo test` / `just ci` could not be
completed here.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix media dependency detection and review findings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: improve context overflow detection and condenser handling (#63)

* fix(openhands): improve context overflow detection and condenser handling

Summary:
- Extract error details from conversation state for better error detection
- Reset conversations when workflow now has condenser but existing doesn't
- Start local server for CLI rehydrate command when needed

Rationale:
- Error detection was only capturing "error" status, not actual error message
- Old conversations without condenser should be reset to get condenser benefits
- CLI rehydrate command now works without a pre-running server

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (all pass)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(openhands): include conversation stats in state mirror for token tracking

Summary:
- Merge conversation.stats into raw_state when applying conversation to state mirror
- This fixes token tracking showing 0 because stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage was not available

Rationale:
- The accumulated_token_usage() function reads from stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage
- OpenHands provides this data in the Conversation.stats field from the REST API
- Without merging stats into raw_state, token tracking would always return None

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (all pass)
- Added state_mirror_includes_stats_from_conversation test

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

* fix(jumia): make refresh_token optional in token schema

Jumia's /token endpoint does not always return refresh_token or
refresh_expires_in. Making these fields optional unblocks the OAuth
flow that was failing with a Zod validation error (502).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): validate refresh token before attempting refresh

Throws a clear 401 error when refresh_token is missing/empty instead
of sending an empty string to Jumia's /token endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add managed HDR loader for DXDemo14

Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

Fix cursor overshoot by serializing SSH input writes

Replace frontend ACK-based input queue with backend serialization.
Each SSH session now has a dedicated write task that processes input
sequentially, eliminating concurrent channel writes that scrambled
input order. Frontend sends fire-and-forget, backend guarantees ordering.

Fixes intermittent cursor position errors during rapid arrow key edits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow (#62)

* fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow

The OpenHands built-in condensation mechanism was not enabled by default,
allowing conversations to exceed the model's maximum context length
(262,143 tokens). This caused ConversationRunError failures that required
manual rehydration.

Changes:
- Enable LLMSummarizingCondenser by default (max_size=240, keep_first=2)
  when the condenser block is omitted from WORKFLOW.md
- Add context overflow error detection (prompt-too-long patterns)
- Auto-recover from context overflow: close old stream, rehydrate to fresh
  conversation with token preservation, retry the turn
- Fix rehydrate_conversation() to return RehydrationFailed with actual
  cause instead of opaque UnexpectedEarlyResult
- Update tests and documentation for new condenser default

* refactor(openhands): extract turn execution and add retry guard

Summary:
- Extract turn execution into execute_turn() helper method
- Extract recovery logic into recover_from_context_overflow() helper
- Add MAX_CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_RETRIES=1 guard to prevent infinite loops
- Reduce nesting from 4+ levels to 2 levels in run_with_observer()

Rationale:
- Addresses PR review feedback on code complexity and infinite retry risk
- Helper methods eliminate code duplication of turn execution flow
- Retry guard ensures at most one recovery attempt per session

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (85 passed)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: 削除モーダルを制御前提に揃える

DeletePaymentModal の非制御利用前提を削除し、open と onClose を必須にした。\nStory も制御前提に揃えてレビュー指摘に対応した。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: MSW の削除レスポンスを簡素化する

payments の MSW ハンドラを成功レスポンス中心の実装に寄せた。\n削除失敗 Story の loader では handler を reset してから override するようにした。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(evals): Stabilize workflow behavior and harness auth (#127)

Stabilize the eval workflow paths that were still failing or proving
brittle. OAuth callback resume now reads persisted thread state
directly, subscribed follow-up routing has clearer guidance for genuine
Junior follow-ups, and the GitHub skill now keeps duplicate checks
silent in the final reply.

This also hardens the harness itself. Vitest env loading now preserves
caller-provided env vars while still letting later .env files override
earlier ones, and eval log capture is scoped to the `slackEval` task so
timed-out scenarios cannot leak gateway-auth failures into later evals.

The branch also adds `pnpm cloudflare:token`, which regenerates the
named Cloudflare tunnel token with `cloudflared` and upserts it into the
root `.env.local`. That makes the stable local Slack tunnel workflow
repeatable after `vercel env pull` rewrites `.env.local`.

Added route-level integration coverage for generic OAuth and MCP OAuth
resume, plus focused unit coverage for env precedence and the eval
harness behavior seam. Locally this passes the targeted OAuth resume
suites, subscribed-message routing suites, env loader tests, eval
harness unit tests, `pnpm --filter @sentry/junior exec tsc --noEmit`,
and a real `pnpm cloudflare:token` refresh for the default `junior-dev`
tunnel. The remaining GitHub skill eval should be rerun outside Codex
against this branch.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Polish media workflows and model setup

- add media metadata, GIF trimming controls, and action gating
- support model setup recovery flows and legacy Whisper directories

Co-authored-by: 이태혁 <127179500+gebalsebal@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics (#929)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Baci
repository.description: Experiment

commit.message:
fix(evals): align guardian learning runtime contracts

Summary:
- update the Batch D eval harness to assert queued and deferred delivery outcomes where evidence-weighted suppression now wins over direct delivery
- refresh runtime eval expectations so generic feedback no longer implies direct or native-channel learning and procedural adaptation expects eight active procedural memories
- record the CI follow-up root cause in the guardian intelligence implementation notes

Rationale:
- backend CI was failing because the eval harness still encoded the pre-Batch D policy contract after delivery arbitration and suppression learning changed runtime behavior
- the shipped code was behaving correctly; the stale assumptions were in harness event lookups and test assertions

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import ast, asyncio, pathlib; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; source=pathlib.Path(tests/test_eval_harness.py).read_text(); mod=ast.parse(source); fn=next(node for node in mod.body if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name==test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details); call=next(n for n in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and getattr(n.func, id, ) == run_runtime_evals); names=[elt.value for elt in call.args[0].elts]; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals(names)); print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed]})'
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import ast, asyncio, pathlib; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; source=pathlib.Path(tests/test_eval_harness.py).read_text(); mod=ast.parse(source); fn=next(node for node in mod.body if isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef) and node.name==test_memory_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details); call=next(n for n in ast.walk(fn) if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and getattr(n.func, id, ) == run_runtime_evals); names=[elt.value for elt in call.args[0].elts]; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals(names)); details={r.name: r.details for r in summary.results}; print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed], active_procedural_memory_count: details[procedural_memory_adaptation_behavior][active_procedural_memory_count]})'
- .venv/bin/python -c 'import asyncio; from src.evals.harness import run_runtime_evals; summary=asyncio.run(run_runtime_evals()); print({failed: summary.failed, errors: [{name: r.name, error: r.error} for r in summary.results if not r.passed]})'
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build

commit.message:
docs: add per-crate readmes (#40)

## Summary
- add dedicated `README.md` files for each workspace crate with
crate-specific descriptions, docs links, badges, feature notes, and
licensing
- wire each crate README into crate-level rustdocs via `#![doc =
include_str!("../README.md")]` so GitHub and generated API docs stay
aligned
- add `description` and `readme` package metadata for every crate, and
include the new README in the `cargo-airbender` package manifest
- update crate dependency snippets to use Git dependencies on the `main`
branch and add an alpha-state warning banner while crates are
unpublished

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `cargo doc --workspace --no-deps --locked` with
`ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo check -p airbender-sdk -p
airbender-host -p cargo-airbender`

## Notes
- plain workspace doc or host-side verification currently needs
`ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true` in this repo because the existing
CUDA-dependent toolchain path cannot determine a local CUDA Toolkit
version

---------

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

commit.message:
fix(network): retry boot node DNS resolution before startup (#1100)

## Summary
- resolve boot node DNS before building the reth network config so reth
does not do the one-shot resolution itself
- retry unresolved boot nodes with `backon` before startup and proceed
once at least one boot node resolves
- add unit coverage for immediate resolution, partial recovery, and the
configured retry budget

## Testing
- cargo test -p zksync_os_network --lib

Replaces closed #1082 on top of current `main`.

commit.message:
fix(guardian): backfill upgrade column and sync success learning

Summary:
- add legacy SQLite backfill and index creation for guardian_interventions.active_project
- refresh procedural learning memories when outcome transitions touch success or failure evidence states
- add regressions for upgraded DBs and delivery-only success synchronization, and record the PR follow-up in implementation docs

Rationale:
- upgraded local databases created before active_project existed would fail ORM reads and writes at runtime
- successful delivered or feedback_received transitions now affect weighted evidence, so durable scoped guidance must refresh on those transitions as well

Tests:
- git diff --check
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/db/engine.py backend/src/guardian/feedback.py backend/tests/test_db_engine.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_learning_arbitration.py backend/tests/test_delivery.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py

commit.message:
fix(memory): isolate scoped procedural guidance bundles

Summary:
- select one winning procedural-guidance scope tier before resolving lessons so broader fallback lanes cannot leak extra biases into a narrower context
- require exact context-key matches on scoped memory reads and persist active_project on guardian interventions so runtime refreshes can write and reload thread/project guidance consistently
- add direct regressions for mixed-scope leakage and record the follow-up in the implementation log

Rationale:
- the first context-aware resolver still merged lessons per axis across thread+project, thread, project, and global scopes
- that let global negative cadence guidance leak into a scoped Atlas bundle and flip a real delivery decision back to bundle

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/memory/procedural_guidance.py backend/tests/test_guardian_feedback.py backend/tests/test_delivery.py backend/tests/test_guardian_state.py
- .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_thread_and_project_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_thread_scope_over_project_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_falls_back_from_thread_project_to_project_to_global tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_prefers_scoped_thread_and_project_context tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_load_procedural_memory_guidance_does_not_mix_broader_lessons_into_selected_scope tests/test_guardian_feedback.py::test_feedback_refresh_writes_thread_and_project_scoped_memories tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_scoped_project_and_thread_guidance_over_global_memory tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_thread_scoped_procedural_guidance_before_project_or_global tests/test_delivery.py::test_deliver_prefers_context_scoped_guidance_over_conflicting_global_memory tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_scoped_project_guidance_over_global_memory tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_thread_scoped_procedural_guidance_before_project_fallback tests/test_guardian_state.py::test_build_guardian_state_prefers_context_scoped_guidance_over_global_memory -q
- cd docs && npm run build

commit.message:
feat(ui): center KPI header and rotate subheads (#13)

Summary:
- update the report title text and center it in the banner
- rotate KPI column labels to 45 degrees using a span wrapper

Rationale:
- improve immediate report identification
- reduce column header readability strain versus vertical labels

Tests:
- rg -n "Tableau récapitulatif des KPIs Nexcer|rotated-label|grid-template-columns: 1fr auto 1fr" static/index.html
- not run (UI walkthrough requires a browser)

Co-authored-by: Wylliam <w.delaunay@groupeonepoint.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

COE-307: Normalize LiteLLM request data into canonical request tables (#20)

* feat(normalization): implement request normalizer job with reconciliation reporting

Summary:
- Create normalize_requests.py with RequestNormalizer and RequestNormalizerJob classes
- Implement ReconciliationReport for tracking unmapped rows with actionable diagnostics
- Add UnmappedRowDiagnostics for detailed per-row failure analysis
- Wire normalization job into CLI with `benchmark normalize run` and `benchmark normalize report` commands
- Export new classes from collectors package
- Add comprehensive unit tests (30 tests covering normalization, reporting, and job execution)

Rationale:
- LiteLLM request data needs normalization into canonical Request ORM entities
- Session correlation keys (experiment_id, variant_id, etc.) must be preserved in metadata
- Idempotent bulk insert via SQLRequestRepository prevents duplicates
- Reconciliation report surfaces unmapped rows with missing field counts and error categories
- CLI integration enables operator-driven normalization workflows

Implementation Details:
- RequestNormalizer handles timestamp parsing (ISO format, Unix epoch, multiple field names)
- Supports latency extraction (seconds->ms conversion, multiple field variants)
- Token counts extracted from nested usage object or top-level fields
- Error detection from error field or response.error
- Cache hit detection from cache_hit or cached fields
- Correlation keys searched in both top-level and nested metadata

Tests:
- All 30 new normalization tests pass
- All 210 existing tests pass
- Lint clean on all new files

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs: Update COE-307 workpad with completion status and PR details

- Mark all acceptance criteria as complete
- Add PR #20 link and status
- Update test count (240 total, 30 new)
- Document lint clean status

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: Address PR feedback for COE-307

Summary:
- Fix error categorization logic to properly classify parse errors
- Refactor dry-run mode to use RequestNormalizer directly (avoid type: ignore)
- Implement proper count query in report command (was stubbed to 0)
- Add verify_normalization.py script for runtime evidence

Rationale:
- PR review requested fixes for type safety, stub implementation, error categorization
- Evidence script demonstrates end-to-end normalization without requiring LiteLLM proxy

Evidence:
- PYTHONPATH=src python scripts/verify_normalization.py shows all demos passing
- 30/30 normalization tests passing
- Lint clean on all modified files

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: Address remaining PR feedback - improved error handling and gitignore

- Fix bulk insert failure handling: now raises RuntimeError instead of conflating with data quality issues
- Add workpad*.md to .gitignore and remove workpad_COE-307.md from tracking
- Update test to expect RuntimeError on repository failure

This separates database/repository failures (infrastructure) from data quality
issues (normalization), allowing proper error handling by the caller.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
